title: Let Every One of You Work Like Stalin!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The original Tamil is reproduced in full below the translation and remains the authoritative text. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement. The statistical claims remain attributed to the reports and institutions cited by Kalaignar. Source-page inspection confirms the damaged debt year as 2015-2016 and the English institutional name represented in Tamil as the National Sample Survey. The Tamil reading copy below repairs only source-confirmed OCR defects; the canonical Tamil source remains unchanged.
paragraphs: As the first stage of campaigning for the Tamil Nadu Legislative Assembly general election to be held on 16 May 2016, Kazhagam Treasurer, younger brother M.K. Stalin, launched the distribution of pamphlets in Chennai's Radhakrishnan Nagar on 8 April 2016. The distribution began to implement a resolution adopted at a meeting of district organisers of the Youth Wing.

Printed on behalf of the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam under the title “A Paralysed Government—A Drowned Tamil Nadu,” the pamphlet explains matters briefly and in an easily understandable way under these subheadings: “A Bankrupt Tamil Nadu,” “A Floundering Tamil Nadu,” “The Scourge of Unemployment,” “Corruption Running Riot,” “A Staggering Tamil Nadu,” “Suffocating Price Rises,” “Law and Order Made a Laughing Stock,” “Farmers on the Brink of Despair,” “AIADMK Election Promises Gone Flat,” and “Empty Announcements under Rule 110.” It ends with the ringing slogan: “Let AIADMK Rule End! Let DMK Rule Dawn!”

I want the Udanpirappugal of every Kazhagam wing—including the Youth Wing, Students' Wing, Women's Wing and Labour Wing—to take this pamphlet to every part of Tamil Nadu through our customary forms of campaigning, such as street-corner and front-veranda campaigns. Meet voters individually, explain the issues to them, and enthusiastically carry out the groundwork required to dispel the darkness of authoritarianism and light the lamp of democracy.

The strength to break the backbone of propaganda from opposition parties and sections of the media that deliberately spread fabricated lies and barren rumours about our Kazhagam is not something it possesses only today or yesterday; it has possessed that strength always. History itself bears witness that our comrades have abundant skill to demolish any conspiracy at its roots and reduce it to dust.

Although it has been only five years, the AIADMK Government has imposed immeasurable misery and boundless suffering upon the people with a cruel nature devoid of the slightest compassion. I hear, like honey flowing into my ears, that hundreds of thousands of comrades—including Kazhagam office-bearers at every level and Kazhagam speakers—long ago began working briskly, wisely and without pause, determined and resolute that this Government must be removed. My heart fills and my memories grow fragrant when I see every Udanpirappu standing upright in the field and proclaiming, “If this army is defeated, what army can win?”

The arrogant and domineering AIADMK rulers may try to create multi-party alliances with the hidden purpose of preventing the votes against them from uniting and instead scattering them. Intelligence personnel and some in the police may support those efforts to any extent. Yet I know fully that, once Kazhagam comrades enter the field with unity of purpose, the enemy's attempt will be crushed before even its point can emerge.

The strength to break the backbone of propaganda from opposition parties and sections of the media that deliberately spread fabricated lies and barren rumours about our Kazhagam is not something it possesses only today or yesterday; it has possessed that strength always. History itself bears witness that our comrades have abundant skill to demolish any conspiracy at its roots and reduce it to dust.

From the very time the DMK was founded, dominant forces have used every opportunity to boast that the DMK has fallen here or perished there. Even while Perarignar Anna led this movement, a few people left the Kazhagam because of differences of opinion, and some muttered, “The Kazhagam is finished.” After Anna's death, some saw the clash of views over leadership of the Kazhagam and predicted that it would “dissolve.” When M.G.R. left, some dreamed that “the Kazhagam will fall apart.”

When the Kazhagam Government was dismissed for the first time and hundreds of its leading members were arrested under MISA and sent to prison, some expected that “repression will make the Kazhagam submit.” After the Kazhagam lost its chance of victory in three consecutive Assembly elections, some said, “The DMK will now collapse, exhausted by defeat.” The Kazhagam rose majestically again, won and governed. Yet when its Government was dismissed a second time, when Rajiv Gandhi was assassinated, and when the Kazhagam suffered a crushing defeat in the election that followed, some eagerly announced, “That is the end of the Kazhagam.” It is by sweeping aside every such prophecy issued over the past fifty years that the Kazhagam now stands as high as the Himalayas, astonishing the entire world.

You understand the need to carry into every town and hamlet, and make resound everywhere, the miseries of five years of AIADMK rule: the murders and robberies across Tamil Nadu; the collapse of law and order; the unemployment tormenting the young; the suffering of mothers caused by liquor shops; the struggles of government employees, nutritious-meal workers and persons with disabilities who seek justice because they have been denied their rightful benefits; the brutal police regime unleashed against those who protest; the bus-fare and electricity-tariff increases imposed upon innocent people after the AIADMK took office; the cruelty of raising the price of milk by sixteen rupees a litre; sky-high prices; and farmers deprived of their livelihoods. These are the conditions through which AIADMK rule has shattered the livelihood of working people at its foundations.

When Perarignar Anna led the Kazhagam, I worked without food or sleep, travelling through every town and hamlet from morning until midnight to campaign, meeting individual Udanpirappugal, speaking with them and encouraging them without pause so that the Kazhagam might grow. Younger brother M.K. Stalin's tireless running about and labour today bring that work back to my memory.
It is not enough for younger brother Stalin alone to work. Every one of you must work like Stalin! Work for the Tamil people! Work with firm confidence! Victory is ours!
Udanpirappē, what should your campaign be like? Must one teach a young fish how to swim? Even so, I wish to underline a few facts for you.

From 2011 to 2015 under Jayalalithaa's rule, 9,948 murders and one lakh robberies and thefts occurred in Tamil Nadu. Every day, seven murders and seventy robberies take place in the State. The situation has deteriorated to the point where one could say that not a day passes without murder or robbery. Crimes against women have also increased. In the past three years alone, 2,335 women have suffered sexual violence. The number of crimes committed against women exceeds twenty thousand.

Mr Punia, Chairman of the National Commission for Scheduled Castes, has alleged that Tamil Nadu is one of the five States with the highest incidence of attacks and sexual violence against Scheduled Castes. In violence against Adi Dravidars from 2011 to April 2015, 213 people were killed and 192 seriously injured. During the same period, 118 incidents of sexual violence occurred. In the past four and a half years alone, 6,014 cases were registered under the Prevention of Atrocities Act.

According to a report published by the Bureau of Police Research and Development, Tamil Nadu has the largest number of protests in India, with 21,232 protests taking place during the past four and a half years. Is it not a grave warning sign that Tamil Nadu ranks first in India for demonstrations and protests expressing social unrest?

Two-crop cultivation in the delta districts has now become an unattainable fruit. The livelihoods of farmers have not been properly protected. The National Crime Records Bureau has pointed out that 2,123 farmers in Tamil Nadu died by suicide.

After the AIADMK came to power, Tamil Nadu's debt burden rose from Rs 1,03,999 crore in 2011-12 to Rs 2,11,482 crore in 2015-16. In terms of personal indebtedness, Tamil Nadu ranks first under AIADMK rule. The National Sample Survey Office has provided statistics showing that personal debt in Tamil Nadu—Rs 45,803 in rural areas and Rs 1,16,404 in urban areas—is higher than in every other State, including West Bengal, Gujarat and Maharashtra.

Since the AIADMK assumed office, it has not independently devised a new electricity-generation project, invited tenders, awarded and completed the work, or generated even a single megawatt of electricity.
Ninety per cent of the promises the AIADMK made during the 2011 general election have not been fulfilled.
The total value of the schemes announced in the 187 statements Jayalalithaa read in the Legislative Assembly under Rule 110 from 2011 to 2016 is Rs 1,72,196.14 crore.

Tamil Nadu Government's average annual revenue receipts are Rs 1,50,000 crore. Chief Minister Jayalalithaa announced under Rule 110 schemes costing more than the Government's revenue receipts for an entire year. Yet not even fifteen per cent of the projects announced under Rule 110 have so far been implemented.

According to the details indicated by statistics, among India's twenty-one large States Tamil Nadu ranks twentieth in overall development, seventeenth in infrastructure, and thirteenth in education. Does this not demonstrate the decline that has occurred in Tamil Nadu's development?

Udanpirappē, compile facts like these and explain each one to everyone you meet each day. Explain them again and again! As you do so, opposition to authoritarianism will swell into an ocean. Go every day from town to town, street to street, house to house and veranda to veranda! Sit with the people on their verandas, drink the water they offer, and campaign!

Continue the election campaign that younger brother M.K. Stalin led and launched in Chennai on 8 April 2016. Meet every voter personally, distribute the pamphlet issued by Kazhagam headquarters, and explain the anti-democratic, anti-people cruelty of AIADMK rule. Resolve to bring AIADMK rule to an end and make Kazhagam rule blossom again!

Kazhagam Treasurer, younger brother Stalin, has already travelled throughout Tamil Nadu meeting people through the carefully planned “Namakku Naame” programme. With the right born of our bond, I ask every Kazhagam Udanpirappu to reproduce that “Namakku Naame” journey in their own area, announce its date and time, and begin immediately.

Continue the election campaign that younger brother M.K. Stalin led and launched in Chennai on 8 April 2016. Meet every voter personally, distribute the pamphlet issued by Kazhagam headquarters, and explain the anti-democratic, anti-people cruelty of AIADMK rule. Resolve to bring AIADMK rule to an end and make Kazhagam rule blossom again!

When Perarignar Anna led the Kazhagam, I worked without food or sleep, travelling through every town and hamlet from morning until midnight to campaign, meeting individual Udanpirappugal, speaking with them and encouraging them without pause so that the Kazhagam might grow. Younger brother M.K. Stalin's tireless running about and labour today bring that work back to my memory.
It is not enough for younger brother Stalin alone to work. Every one of you must work like Stalin! Work for the Tamil people! Work with firm confidence! Victory is ours!
With affection,
M.K.
